frameworks.name: Market jobs and barriers
frameworks.summary: Use to explain buyer urgency, switching friction, and trust barriers.
applicability_rules: Use before making broad category claims.
evidence_expectations: Keep one buyer barrier and one proof requirement visible.
default_when: The wedge is still being defined.

frameworks.name: Signal-weighted priority
frameworks.summary: Use to rank opportunities and features with visible tradeoffs.
applicability_rules: Use when one next slice must be chosen explicitly.
evidence_expectations: Keep source artifact ids and burden visible.
default_when: The workspace needs a ranked next move.

frameworks.name: Competitive displacement map
frameworks.summary: Use to compare ProductOS against named competitors, status quo, and in-house alternatives.
applicability_rules: Use when challenger positioning and displacement barriers matter.
evidence_expectations: Every competitor row should carry at least one evidence ref.
default_when: The PM must justify a wedge.
avoid_when: No bounded comparison can be made yet.

selection_policies.selection_logic: Use market jobs and barriers first when trust and switching friction shape the decision.
